target.name: Sila Nanotechnologies
target.founder: Gleb Yushin, Alex Jacobs, Gene Berdichevsky

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $3.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.8B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sila Nanotechnologies is a late-stage Climate & Energy company founded by Gleb Yushin, Alex Jacobs, and Gene Berdichevsky, currently valued at $3.3B post-money on $375M raised. The valuation triangulation leverages a comparables universe of 9 peers (1 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector and stage, including TAE Technologies, Form Energy, Ascend Elements, and Watershed.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression, using a typical 1.2x step-up multiplier from the last post-money valuation, implies $4.0B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produced a $3.2B implied value, with the peer set ranging from $1.8B (Watershed) to $6.0B (TAE Technologies). The median valuation across peer comps sits at $3.5B and $3.0B for the two closest stage matches.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $2.8B to $4.6B, with a median of $3.6B. The current $3.3B valuation positions Sila near the lower quartile of this range and below the peer median of $3.2B-$3.5B. Key uncertainties include technology commercialization timeline, capital intensity to revenue inflection, and relative competitive position within the advanced battery materials segment. Stage-progression and peer methodology show 250 basis point divergence, reflecting heterogeneity in comparable company maturity and financing paths.
